name: Chicken Chow Mein
recipeIngredient: 1 package dried or fresh Chinese noodles, 12 oz
1 chicken breast, cut into thin strips
1 small head bok choy, leaves and stalks, thinly sliced
1 container fresh bean sprouts, rinsed and drained (about 1-1/2 cups)
1 cup mushrooms, shiitake if possible, cut into small pieces
1 tablespoon black bean paste or sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Mix together the first soy sauce, rice wine vinegar, and cornstarch in a bowl.
2. Marinate the chicken strips in this mixture for at least 30 minutes.
3. Cook the noodles in boiling water until tender. Drain well.
4. In another bowl, mix together the second soy sauce, oyster sauce, black bean paste, and sugar.
5. Heat a large wok over high heat. Add half a tablespoon of peanut oil when the wok is smoking.
6. Stir-fry the vegetables briefly, then transfer them to a plate and set aside.
7. Reheat the wok with the remaining oil. Add the marinated chicken and stir-fry until the meat is cooked through.
8. Add the soy sauce mixture and bring to a boil.
9. Add the cooked noodles and vegetables back to the wok. Toss well to coat everything evenly.
10. Serve hot, optionally alongside additional chow mein noodles if desired.
